release_review.summary: Desktop embedded drag-region synchronization now ignores Workbench subtree mutations that cannot affect window chrome drag or no-drag regions.
checked_surfaces: Desktop embedded drag-region mutation routing only schedules snapshot refreshes for top-bar, explicit drag-root, or explicit no-drag marker changes
Workbench subtree mutations outside drag-region anchors no longer force repeated drag-region DOM scans or layout reads
ResizeObserver-backed geometry refreshes, titlebar drag roots, and no-drag blocker behavior continue to use the existing Desktop embedded drag-region host contract
Desktop preload drag-region host messages keep the same snapshot payload shape and continue to accept renderer refreshes through the existing bridge

Env App Workbench wheel ownership, text selection ownership, widget pointer routing, runtime-control, Local UI health, startup reports, provider link, and desktop bridge protocol fields keep the same compatibility window
release_review.same_window_rationale: The release changes only Desktop renderer-side drag-region refresh scheduling. It does not add, remove, or change any Runtime Service JSON fields, protocol version, runtime-control endpoint, startup report shape, Local UI health surface, provider-link surface, or desktop bridge framing. Existing Desktop hosts continue to receive the same drag-region snapshot contract, with fewer irrelevant Workbench mutation-triggered refreshes.
